commit.message:
Fix false positives in exploit/scan modules (FW3.7.1)

- Add _discover_params(): crawl real page params before testing
- SQLi: use baseline comparison to filter pre-existing SQL-like text
- SQLi: time-based threshold now requires +4s vs baseline (not raw 2.5s)
- XSS: use unique session marker instead of generic payloads
- XSS: remove DOM sink false positives (innerHTML/eval exist everywhere)
- CMDi: use echo CMDI_CONFIRMED marker, remove generic 'ttl' indicator
- LFI: remove empty indicator payload (matched everything)
- LFI: use specific indicators (root:x:0:0:, HTTP_HOST=)
- vBulletin: require 'vbulletin' in response body (not just 302)
- upload_check: only report 200 with upload form content (not 403)
- sqli_scan: discover real params from page before injecting

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Release WebGate FW3.7

New features:
- SSH backdoor via RCE CVE with Scope Agreement (typed 'I accept')
- TOR routing via bridge (obfs4/webtunnel/snowflake)
- SQLMap interactive dump dialog: pick DB -> table -> dump in GUI
- CVEExploitOfferDialog: auto-prompt when RCE CVE detected after scan
- ScopeAgreementDialog: typed 'I accept' confirmation before exploit
- SSHBackdoorDialog: configure SSH credentials + TOR bridge
- SQLMapDumpDialog: GUI-based DB/table selector with background dump

Improvements:
- Simplified reports: SQL injection Y/N + vulnerable param/payload
- XSStrike output simplified to injection Y/N + vulnerable URL
- Progress bar with gradient, % overlay, bright pulse edge animation
- Logo glitch animation (random interval glitch effect)
- Particle burst animation on scan start
- FW badge in logo canvas
- Scan-line border animation during active scan

Fixes:
- Removed COPY button (caused GUI freeze/lag on Wayland)
- Version strings updated to FW3.7
- LICENSE added (authorized use only)
- README.md fully rewritten with all features documented

commit.message:
Fix fallback logic: no network uses existing config, not hardcoded

Priority order:
1. Has network → fetch latest, let user choose recommended vs current
2. No network + has existing config → use existing directly (no prompt)
3. No network + fresh install → last resort hardcoded fallback

commit.message:
Refactor mobile process page to pre-built GSAP timeline

Root cause: iOS Safari fires scroll events 1-2 frames behind the native
scroll, so manual per-frame DOM writes in onUpdate always lag. Homepage
animations are time-driven (rAF/CSS) and run on the GPU compositor,
which is why they're smooth — scroll-linked JS animations are not.

Fix: on mobile, pre-build all keyframes (inner.y, strokeDashoffset,
dot cx/cy, card triggers) into a single GSAP timeline and attach it
via ScrollTrigger's `animation` property. GSAP handles all interpolation
internally through its optimized render loop, which syncs far better
with iOS's scroll pipeline than manual calculations in onUpdate.

Desktop keeps the manual onUpdate for full visual fidelity (dot pulse
ring, per-frame color interpolation, smooth scrub lerp).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Make Places API daily limit dynamic based on days in month

Instead of a static stored limit, compute it as $200 / $0.032 / daysInMonth.
This gives 201 calls/day in 31-day months and 223/day in 28-day months.

commit.message:
Add Step 3 domain model, store layer, and acceptance test scaffold (#3)

* Add Step 3 domain model, store layer, and acceptance test scaffold

Domain model (internal/domain/program.go):
- Program, ProgramWorkout, Section, SectionExercise, ProgressionRule structs
- ProgressionStrategy value object (linear, percentage, wave)
- Validate() on all entities with business rule enforcement
- Program.HasDayNumber() invariant check for duplicate day_numbers
- Program.DeepCopy() for full tree copy with new UUIDs/timestamps
- ProgressionRule.NextWeight() with linear, percentage, wave, and deload

Store layer:
- program_store.go: CRUD, GetProgramWithTree (5-query tree assembly),
  ListPrograms with is_template filter, HasActiveCycle, CopyProgram
- workout_store.go: Workout/Section/SectionExercise/ProgressionRule CRUD,
  reorder operations, parent ownership verification, reindexSortOrder helper

Acceptance tests:
- 21 Gherkin scenarios in programs.feature covering program CRUD,
  workouts, sections, section exercises, reorder, deep copy, active cycle lock
- program_steps.go with all step definitions and UUID caches
- Extended TestClient with program/workout/section/exercise UUID maps

Domain unit tests (program_test.go):
- Validation tests for all 5 entities
- DeepCopy independence and UUID uniqueness tests
- NextWeight calculation tests (linear, percentage, wave, deload)
- HasDayNumber conflict detection

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Convert all Gherkin tables to header-row format

Addresses PR review feedback: all Cucumber tables now use
header-row format (| header | ... | / | value | ... |) instead of
key-value columns. Refactored tableToMap to delegate to
tableToMapSlice and updated theResponseShouldInclude to use it.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Document @wip workflow and header-row table convention in testing docs

- Tag programs.feature with @wip so the suite skips it while the
  handler layer is not yet built; tests re-enable when @wip is removed
- Add Tags: "~@wip" to godog options so make test stays green on
  branches with in-progress features
- Update testing-strategy.md: BDD flow now shows the tag/untag cycle,
  Gherkin examples use header-row format, and a new "Data table format"
  section explains why header rows are required over header columns

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(api): add validation_completeness, layers_executed, layers_skipped to /v1/validate

Contextualizes `valid: true` with the depth of validation actually performed.
When Saxon or schemas are missing, the response now reports `partial` instead
of silently returning a potentially misleading `valid: true`.

New fields (backward-compatible defaults):
- validation_completeness: "full" | "partial"
- layers_executed: ["xsd", "schematron", "pdfa3b", "br_fr_ctc"]
- layers_skipped: [{"layer": "...", "reason": "..."}]

211 tests pass (208 existing + 3 new).

feat: autonomous core — goal decomposition, task queue, self-reflection

Angelina is now an autonomous agent, not just a chatbot:

- /api/worker/reflect: weekly self-analysis reviews patterns and generates strategy adjustments

- Graceful fallback: works with or without autonomous tables

commit.message:
Use exec adbd to keep it alive as app child process (v2.0)

Background & approach: every init-based method of starting adbd has
failed on this device. Root cause: the & + exit pattern causes adbd to
receive SIGHUP when the su shell exits.

Fix: use 'exec <adbd>' which replaces the su shell with adbd directly.
The Java Process object (adbdProcess field) keeps adbd alive as a child
of the app process. Also locate the binary dynamically (/sbin/adbd or
/system/bin/adbd).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Streak card: full-card Doom fire background, tiered by streak

Add CardFireAnimation (W=80×H=50 Doom canvas) as an absolute
background layer on the streak card. Fire intensity scales with
streak:
  0–2  days → gradient card only (no canvas)
  3–6  days → seedHeat 195, flames fill lower ~60% of card
  7–13 days → seedHeat 220, lower ~85%
  14–29     → seedHeat 240, nearly full card
  30+       → seedHeat 255, full inferno

A dark-to-transparent gradient overlay (z-index 1) keeps the
streak number, stats and button readable over the live fire.
All text content sits in .daily-checkin-content at z-index 2.
Card gets position:relative + overflow:hidden to anchor the
canvas and clip it to the rounded corners.

commit.message:
Rewrite navbar with premium scroll-driven floating pill transition

Major improvements to the sticky navbar animation:

- Replace transition-all with granular per-property transitions
  using inline styles for buttery-smooth interpolation
- Use scroll progress (0-1 over 80px) instead of binary toggle
  for continuous padding/border-radius animation
- Apple-style spring easing: cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1)
- Frosted glass: backdrop-blur(20px) + saturate(1.8) with
  semi-transparent oklch background
- Subtle inset highlight shadow for depth on the floating pill
- Animated hamburger icon (Menu rotates out, X rotates in)
- Mobile menu uses CSS grid collapse (grid-template-rows: 0fr→1fr)
  for smooth height animation without fixed heights
- Mobile links auto-close menu on click
- Divider between nav items and CTAs for visual separation

Co-Authored-By: Claude (claude-opus-4-6) <noreply@anthropic.com>
